perturbation.original_text: Article 1 – Definition of Confidential and Proprietary Information “Confidential Information” means any information or data, or both, communicated by or on behalf of the ICC Court to the Member, including, but not limited to, any kind of business, commercial, technical, legal, financial information and data in connection with the arbitral proceedings, the activities and image of the ICC Court, except for information that is demonstrabl y non -confidential in nature. The information shall be Confidential Information, irrespective of the medium in which that information or data is embedded, and whether the Confidential Information is disclosed orally, visually or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 1 – Definition of Confidential and Proprietary Information “Confidential Information” means any information or data, or both, communicated by or on behalf of the ICC Court to the Member, including, but not limited to, any kind of business, commercial, technical, legal, financial information and data in connection with the arbitral proceedings, the activities and image of the ICC Court, except for information that is demonstrabl y non -confidential in nature. The information shall NOT be Confidential Information, irrespective of the medium in which that information or data is embedded, and whether the Confidential Information is disclosed orally, visually or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'The information shall be Confidential Information' to 'The information shall NOT be Confidential Information' in Article 1 creates a direct contradiction within the definition of "Confidential Information." The first part of the definition describes what constitutes confidential information, while the changed sentence states that the information is not confidential, creating a contradiction. This could make the obligations unclear, and whether the member is obligated to restrict its use as specified in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 1 – Definition of Confidential and Proprietary Information “Confidential Information” means any information or data, or both, communicated by or on behalf of the ICC Court to the Member, including, but not limited to, any kind of business, commercial, technical, legal, financial information and data in connection with the arbitral proceedings, the activities and image of the ICC Court, except for information that is demonstrabl y non -confidential in nature. The information shall NOT be Confidential Information, irrespective of the medium in which that information or data is embedded, and whether the Confidential Information is disclosed orally, visually or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'The information shall be Confidential Information' to 'The information shall NOT be Confidential Information' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts the initial statement that the information *is* confidential. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the definition itself, as well as in the agreement as a whole. The Contradicted Text and Changed text are in the same location in this case, but the Change creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the definition now includes conflicting statements about the nature of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means execution of reasonable measures to return or destroy all materials and copies including electronic data. Destruction shall be confirmed in writing. Disposa l shall be effected within thirty (30) days of the request being made.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means execution of reasonable measures to return or destroy all materials and copies including electronic data. Destruction shall NOT be confirmed in writing. Disposa l shall be effected within thirty (30) days of the request being made.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying 'Destruction shall be confirmed in writing' to 'Destruction shall NOT be confirmed in writing', Article 12 now specifies that a confirmation of destruction, which is necessary to prove compliance, will not be provided. This contradicts the need for proof of compliance, making enforcement more uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means execution of reasonable measures to return or destroy all materials and copies including electronic data. Destruction shall NOT be confirmed in writing. Disposa l shall be effected within thirty (30) days of the request being made.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Article 12 such that 'Destruction shall NOT be confirmed in writing.' The original text stated 'Destruction shall be confirmed in writing'. The change directly contradicts the need for proof of compliance, as explained in the perturbation explanation, which is necessary to prove compliance as stated in the definition, making enforcement more uncertain because there is no evidence that it was destroyed, which is an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is also in Article 12.

perturbation.original_text: Article 2 – Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The Member shall: a) not disclose any Confidential Information or Proprie tary Information unless expressly authorised by the ICC Court; b) use any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information exclusively for the purpose of its collaboration with the ICC Court and not for its own or anyone else’s benefit; c) keep secu re, confidential and hold all Confidential Information and Proprietary Information with no less a degree of care as is used for the Member’s own confidential or Proprietary Information and at least with reasonable care; and d) not amend, vary, develop, im prove, enhance (“Derivation”) the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except as otherwise approved in writing by the ICC Court.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 2 – Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The Member shall: a) disclose any Confidential Information or Proprie tary Information unless expressly authorised by the ICC Court; b) use any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information exclusively for the purpose of its collaboration with the ICC Court and not for its own or anyone else’s benefit; c) keep secu re, confidential and hold all Confidential Information and Proprietary Information with no less a degree of care as is used for the Member’s own confidential or Proprietary Information and at least with reasonable care; and d) not amend, vary, develop, im prove, enhance (“Derivation”) the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except as otherwise approved in writing by the ICC Court.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'not disclose any Confidential Information' to 'disclose any Confidential Information' creates a direct contradiction. The member is obligated to disclose all confidential information unless authorized by the ICC Court, contradicting the intent of a non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 2 – Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The Member shall: a) disclose any Confidential Information or Proprie tary Information unless expressly authorised by the ICC Court; b) use any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information exclusively for the purpose of its collaboration with the ICC Court and not for its own or anyone else’s benefit; c) keep secu re, confidential and hold all Confidential Information and Proprietary Information with no less a degree of care as is used for the Member’s own confidential or Proprietary Information and at least with reasonable care; and d) not amend, vary, develop, im prove, enhance (“Derivation”) the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except as otherwise approved in writing by the ICC Court.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Article 2(a) from 'not disclose any Confidential Information' to 'disclose any Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the fundamental principle of a confidentiality agreement, creating a clear and undeniable in-text contradiction within the same Article 2. The instruction to disclose information clashes directly with the expectation of confidentiality, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage. ICC therefore reserves the right to take whatever injunctive or other action it deems necessary, at law, in equity or otherwise to protect its interests in the event of any actual and/or alleged breach of this Agreement by the Member.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage. ICC therefore does NOT reserve the right to take whatever injunctive or other action it deems necessary, at law, in equity or otherwise to protect its interests in the event of any actual and/or alleged breach of this Agreement by the Member.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'ICC therefore reserves the right to take whatever injunctive or other action' to 'ICC therefore does NOT reserve the right to take whatever injunctive or other action' in Article 11, it creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence describes the detrimental effects of a breach, implying that ICC would seek remedies, while the modified sentence says ICC does not reserve the right to act on it. This renders the clause meaningless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage. ICC therefore does NOT reserve the right to take whatever injunctive or other action it deems necessary, at law, in equity or otherwise to protect its interests in the event of any actual and/or alleged breach of this Agreement by the Member.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Article 11, removing ICC's right to take action in the event of a breach, directly contradicting the initial statement in the same article that such breaches may be highly prejudicial to ICC's interests. This creates a clear inconsistency because the detrimental effects are acknowledged, but the ability to seek remedies is explicitly denied. The contradicted text is the changed Article 11 itself. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, specifically when a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, the same location) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Article 7 – No Warranty The ICC Court makes available the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information as is and only warrants that, it has done its best efforts to ensure to the best possible extent that the information disclosed is complete, accurate, free from defects or third party rights, and useful for the purposes of the Member.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 7 – No Warranty The ICC Court makes available the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information as is and does NOT warrant that, it has done its best efforts to ensure to the best possible extent that the information disclosed is complete, accurate, free from defects or third party rights, and useful for the purposes of the Member.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'only warrants that, it has done its best efforts' to 'does NOT warrant that, it has done its best efforts', Article 7 creates a contradiction on the warranty being provided. At first it states that, while not providing a full warranty, that best efforts have been made, now its denying that those best efforts were even made. This introduces uncertainty about what is the liability to the information provided, and if best efforts have been made or not
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 7 – No Warranty The ICC Court makes available the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information as is and does NOT warrant that, it has done its best efforts to ensure to the best possible extent that the information disclosed is complete, accurate, free from defects or third party rights, and useful for the purposes of the Member.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the ICC Court 'only warrants that, it has done its best efforts'. The changed text states that the ICC Court 'does NOT warrant that, it has done its best efforts'. This creates a direct contradiction. The original implies that best efforts were made, while the changed text denies that those efforts were made. Since the contradicted text is the changed text, it means the contradiction is happening in the same location. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. In this case, the initial intent of a warranty of best efforts is directly negated, thus causing a strong contradiction.
